paper_title: Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?

claims.claim_text: Existing evaluations fail to evaluate many representative memory architectures under unified workloads, making principled cross-system comparisons difficult

evidence.quote_text: First, they fail to evaluate many representative memory architectures (e.g., systems such as MemoChat, MemTree, and LightMem have not been included in prior evaluations) under unified workloads, making principled cross-system comparisons difficult.

claims.claim_text: Database community efforts limit their scope to a few chatbot-centric datasets, neglecting complex agentic execution scenarios

evidence.quote_text: Efforts from the database community (Wu et al., 2026) limit their scope to a few chatbot-centric datasets (e.g., LoCoMo and LongMemEval only), neglecting complex agentic execution scenarios.

claims.claim_text: Existing benchmarks rely on single-sided end-to-end metrics rather than multi-dimensional evaluation

evidence.quote_text: existing benchmarks predominantly rely on single-sided, end-to-end task success metrics (e.g., F1 and BLEU scores) rather than a comprehensive evaluation suite. They fail to explicitly isolate and measure multi-dimensional performance indicators such as evidence-level retrieval fidelity, dynamic update robustness under conflicting knowledge, and long-horizon stability.

claims.claim_text: Existing benchmarks rarely measure key operational costs such as index construction time and query latency

evidence.quote_text: Third, they rarely measure key operational costs from a systems perspective, such as index construction time and query latency, which are critical for production deployments.

claims.claim_text: Existing evaluations treat memory systems as monolithic black boxes rather than decomposing them into fundamental data management modules

evidence.quote_text: they treat memory systems as monolithic black boxes rather than decomposing them into fundamental data management modules for isolated, fine-grained analysis.

claims.claim_text: The paper decomposes existing agent memory systems into four core components: representation and storage, extraction, retrieval and routing, and maintenance

evidence.quote_text: We decompose existing agent memory systems into four core components: (i) memory representation and storage, (ii) memory extraction, (iii) memory retrieval and routing, and (iv) memory maintenance.

claims.claim_text: The paper evaluates 12 representative memory systems across five benchmark workloads encompassing 11 datasets

evidence.quote_text: We conduct end-to-end evaluations under a unified and fair testbed across five distinct benchmark workloads encompassing 11 datasets. Our study includes 12 representative memory systems.

claims.claim_text: The paper conducts fine-grained controlled experiments within each technique component using controlled variants

evidence.quote_text: By systematically generating controlled variants that modify one module at a time, we quantify their performance trade-offs and assess their individual impacts on representation fidelity, routing precision, and update correctness.

claims.claim_text: Agent memory system is formalized as a tuple of four modules: M_sys = ⟨R, S, Q, U⟩

evidence.quote_text: we formalize the agent memory system as a tuple of four modules: M_sys = ⟨R, S, Q, U⟩, where each module governs a distinct phase of the memory lifecycle.

claims.claim_text: Existing agent memory systems span four architectural paradigms: Stream-and-Reflection, Hierarchical Tiered, Knowledge Graph, and Composite Hybrid

evidence.quote_text: (1) Stream-and-Reflection Memory System... (2) Hierarchical Tiered Memory System... (3) Knowledge Graph Memory System... (4) Composite Hybrid Memory System...

claims.claim_text: No single memory system dominates all workloads; methods that preserve task-critical evidence through structure-guided filtering remain the most competitive overall

evidence.quote_text: No single memory system dominates all workloads, but methods that preserve task-critical evidence through structure-guided filtering remain the most competitive overall.

claims.claim_text: Structure-aware systems lead LongMemEval: Zep reaches 48.0 LLM Judge Accuracy and Cognee attains 35.3 ROUGE-L F1

evidence.quote_text: Structure-aware systems lead LongMemEval, where Zep reaches 48.0 LLM Judge Accuracy and Cognee attains 35.3 ROUGE-L F1

claims.claim_text: Hybrid filtering is strongest on LoCoMo exactness, where MemOS reaches 11.5 Exact Match (EM)

evidence.quote_text: Hybrid filtering is strongest on LoCoMo exactness, where MemOS reaches 11.5 Exact Match (EM)

claims.claim_text: Trace-preserving memories remain strongest on DB-Bench: Long Context achieves 48.20 EM and MemoChat 55.40 Task Success Rate

evidence.quote_text: Trace-preserving memories remain strongest on DB-Bench, where Long Context achieves 48.20 EM and MemoChat reaches 55.40 Task Success Rate

claims.claim_text: Retrieval quality depends more on how a system organizes evidence for later reconstruction than on how well it ranks one relevant memory first

evidence.quote_text: retrieval quality depends more on how a system organizes evidence for later reconstruction than on how well it ranks one relevant memory first

claims.claim_text: A-MEM and MemTree become clearly stronger at larger retrieval budgets, reaching 69.5/85.9 and 59.7/80.5 on Recall@5/@10

evidence.quote_text: A-MEM and MemTree become clearly stronger at larger retrieval budgets, reaching 69.5/85.9 and 59.7/80.5 on Recall@5/@10

claims.claim_text: Reliable post-update behavior is a pipeline-level design problem rather than a pure model-capacity problem

evidence.quote_text: reliable post-update behavior is a pipeline-level design problem rather than a pure model-capacity problem

claims.claim_text: Zep leads Knowledge Update with 44.4 Substring EM and 36.8 ROUGE-L F1

evidence.quote_text: Zep leads Knowledge Update with 44.4 Substring EM and 36.8 ROUGE-L F1

claims.claim_text: Cognee leads Temporal Reasoning with 18.7 Substring EM and 35.8 ROUGE-L F1

evidence.quote_text: Cognee leads Temporal Reasoning with 18.7 Substring EM and 35.8 ROUGE-L F1

claims.claim_text: Flat context accumulation or dense similarity alone is weakest when stale mentions must be separated from updated ones

evidence.quote_text: flat context accumulation or dense similarity alone is weakest when stale mentions must be separated from updated ones

claims.claim_text: Backbone variation changes absolute answer quality more than which memory pipeline remains effective; stable update behavior is determined before final generation

evidence.quote_text: Backbone variation changes absolute answer quality more than it changes which memory pipeline remains effective, indicating that stable update behavior is determined primarily before final generation.

claims.claim_text: Memory remains more stable at longer horizons when evidence is organized through explicit relational links or hierarchical consolidation rather than left as flat text

evidence.quote_text: Memory remains more stable at longer horizons when evidence is organized through explicit relational links or hierarchical consolidation, rather than left as flat text for direct matching.

claims.claim_text: SimpleMem stays nearly unchanged from Short to Medium buckets (35.2 to 34.9 Accuracy) while Long Context drops from 42.6 to 19.0

evidence.quote_text: SimpleMem stays nearly unchanged from the Short to Medium buckets (35.2 to 34.9 Accuracy), whereas Long Context drops from 42.6 to 19.0

claims.claim_text: Embedding RAG falls from 37.1 to 7.4 Answer F1 as evidence gap widens, while graph-based systems remain substantially higher

evidence.quote_text: Embedding RAG falls from 37.1 to 7.4 Answer F1 as the evidence gap widens, while graph- or consolidated-memory systems such as Cognee, MemOS, and MemoryOS remain substantially higher across the same bins

claims.claim_text: Efficiency is governed by maintenance scope rather than structure alone

evidence.quote_text: efficiency is governed by maintenance scope rather than structure alone

claims.claim_text: LightMem and MemTree occupy the strongest efficiency frontier: LightMem at 48.3 Normalized Utility at 3.67s, MemTree at 63.5 at 15.9s

evidence.quote_text: LightMem and MemTree occupy the strongest efficiency frontier, with LightMem reaching 48.3 Normalized Utility at 3.67s Avg. Operation Latency/Query and MemTree reaching 63.5 at 15.9s

claims.claim_text: Cognee and Zep exceed 84 utility only after 116.5s and 155.1s respectively

evidence.quote_text: Cognee and Zep exceed 84 utility only after 116.5s and 155.1s

claims.claim_text: Segmented compression and bounded hybrid retrieval keep LightMem in the low-cost regime; path-local tree aggregation allows MemTree to preserve utility without global refresh

evidence.quote_text: segmented compression and bounded hybrid retrieval keep LightMem close to the low-cost regime; (2) path-local tree aggregation allows MemTree to preserve substantially more utility without global refresh

claims.claim_text: Retaining the original conversational content is more important than increasing abstraction or hierarchy for sustaining both factual recall and reasoning quality

evidence.quote_text: Retaining the original conversational content is more important than increasing abstraction or hierarchy for sustaining both factual recall and reasoning quality.

claims.claim_text: LightMem User-Only Raw achieves the best result on all four metrics (24.2 EM, 38.9 Ans. F1 on LoCoMo; 26.0 Substr. EM, 31.4 ROUGE-L F1 on LongMemEval)

evidence.quote_text: LightMem User-Only Raw achieves the best result on all four metrics

claims.claim_text: Coverage-preserving write-time extraction provides the most stable balance between factual retrieval and downstream reasoning

evidence.quote_text: Coverage-preserving write-time extraction provides the most stable balance between factual retrieval and downstream reasoning

claims.claim_text: Retrieval quality improves most from targeted structure rather than added complexity

evidence.quote_text: retrieval quality improves most from targeted structure rather than added complexity

claims.claim_text: Conservative consolidation is more effective than delayed flushing or overly coarse summarization for maintaining answer-relevant memory

evidence.quote_text: Conservative consolidation is more effective than delayed flushing or overly coarse summarization for maintaining answer-relevant memory
